description: Tailored specifically for internship/WIL applications. Emphasises learning goals, academic relevance, and short-term contribution.

examples.title: Internship Application at a Law Firm
examples.body:
8 March 2026

Dear Mr. Siwale,

I am writing to apply for the legal internship position at your esteemed law firm. I am currently in my fourth year of the Bachelor of Laws programme at the University of Zambia, and I am seeking practical experience that will complement my academic studies and prepare me for the legal profession.

My academic performance has been strong, with particular strengths in contract law, constitutional law, and legal drafting. I have completed coursework in legal research methodology, case analysis, and statutory interpretation. I am also proficient in legal citation and have experience using electronic legal databases such as LexisNexis and the Zambia Legal Information Institute.

I am eager to learn from experienced legal practitioners about the day-to-day realities of legal practice. I understand that an internship involves a significant amount of research, document preparation, and case file management, and I am prepared to approach these responsibilities with diligence and attention to detail. I am particularly interested in gaining exposure to commercial law and dispute resolution.

I am available for a three-month internship from June to August 2026, and I am willing to work full-time. I am committed to maintaining the highest standards of professional ethics and confidentiality.

I would be grateful for the opportunity to contribute to your firm while developing the practical skills that will shape my legal career.

Yours sincerely,

Ruth Mwanza

examples.title: WIL Application at National Food and Nutrition Commission
examples.body:
15 May 2026

Dear Dr. Tembo,

I am applying for the Work-Integrated Learning placement in the Nutrition Programmes Division at the National Food and Nutrition Commission. I am a third-year student at the University of Zambia studying Food and Nutrition Science, and I am passionate about addressing malnutrition and food insecurity in our communities.

My academic training has covered nutrition assessment, food safety, community nutrition interventions, and public health nutrition policy. I have also completed practical modules in dietary analysis using NutriSurvey software and anthropometric measurement techniques. I am familiar with WHO growth standards and the National Food and Nutrition Policy framework.

During a community nutrition project in my second year, I worked with a team to conduct dietary surveys in two peri-urban communities. We collected and analysed 24-hour dietary recall data from 60 households, identified micronutrient deficiencies, and proposed community-based fortification strategies. Our project report was commended by our lecturers for its data quality and practical recommendations.

I am particularly interested in your commission's school feeding programmes and micronutrient supplementation initiatives. I believe that hands-on experience in these areas will deepen my understanding of how nutrition policies translate into real-world impact.

I am available for a six-month WIL placement starting in July 2026. I am committed to learning, contributing, and upholding the professional standards expected of public health nutrition practitioners.

Yours faithfully,

Esther Mwape

examples.title: Industrial Attachment at Zambezi Portland Cement
examples.body:
2 February 2026

Dear Human Resources Manager,

I am writing to apply for the industrial attachment position in the Production Department at Zambezi Portland Cement. I am a fourth-year Chemical Engineering student at Copperbelt University, and I am seeking practical industrial experience in process engineering and manufacturing operations.

My academic programme has provided me with a solid foundation in chemical process design, thermodynamics, material and energy balances, and process control. I have completed laboratory work in reaction engineering, separation processes, and process simulation using Aspen Plus. I have also studied environmental engineering, which is particularly relevant to the cement industry's focus on emissions reduction and sustainability.

I am particularly interested in cement manufacturing because it combines multiple engineering disciplines, mechanical, chemical, and environmental, in a single integrated process. I am eager to learn about raw material processing, kiln operations, quality control protocols, and energy efficiency measures. My coursework in heat transfer and combustion engineering has prepared me to understand the thermal processes central to cement production.

I am a hardworking, safety-conscious student who takes pride in following procedures and contributing to team objectives. During a factory tour in my third year, I was impressed by your facility's cleanliness, organisation, and commitment to worker safety. I would be proud to learn from your experienced engineers and technicians.

I am available for a twelve-month industrial attachment commencing in June 2026.

Yours faithfully,

Paul Kasongo
